Both commands follow the exact same pattern as `create.ts`:

userStories.title: Registry declares `edit image` and `upscale image` models and scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `registry.ts` to be the single source of truth for edit/upscale model names and their script paths so that adding future models only requires editing one file.

acceptanceCriteria.text: A new `EDIT_IMAGE_SCRIPTS` map is added, keyed by model name, with the following values:
`getScript("edit", "image", model)` returns the correct entry from `EDIT_IMAGE_SCRIPTS`.
`getScript("upscale", "image", model)` returns the correct entry from

userStories.title: `edit image` action wires up flux and qwen models
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `parallax edit image --model flux_4b_base --prompt "..." --input photo.jpg` to actually invoke the Python script so that I can edit images from the terminal.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The `edit image` action handler follows the pattern:
`--input` is a `requiredOption` and is validated to exist on disk before invoking the
`buildEditImageArgs` maps CLI options to Python script args per model:
Running `parallax edit image --model qwen --prompt "change background" --input photo.jpg`

userStories.title: New `upscale image` command wires up esrgan and latent_upscale
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `parallax upscale image --model esrgan --checkpoint model.safetensors --esrgan-checkpoint RealESRGAN_x4plus.safetensors --prompt "..."` to invoke the upscale Python script so that I can upscale images from the terminal.

userStories.title: `index.ts` registers the upscale command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `parallax upscale` to appear as a valid top-level command so that it is discoverable via `parallax --help`.
